summary:
The process of calcifying a third molar typically starts by about one and a half years; however, it may take several more decades before these teeth fully form due to their delayed eruption timeline post-adolescence. Key is recognizing this early start but prolonged development phase characteristic of wisdom tooth formation processes. To directly answer your query regarding when calcification initiates specifically—this occurs approximately during infancy as per current medical understanding based upon dental literature reviewed here today. Agent2 concludes with confidence after reviewing multiple sources indicating initial stages occur very young yet completion happens later often outside typical pediatric care settings where such issues might be addressed clinically if problematic arises from incomplete mineralisation leading up towards late teens/early twenties usually depending heavily individual variation factors including genetics etc..
